key_insight: A phase-field fracture model with overdamped mechanics can reproduce the transition from continuous sea ice to fragmented granular ice under tensile and shear loading.
engineering_relevance: This provides a computationally tractable way to represent sea-ice breakup and damage in regional and climate-scale models, improving predictions of ice dynamics.
method: Double-well phase-field free energy coupled to overdamped displacement response
Spectral Fourier-space solver with body-force loading
Validated on tensile inclusion opening, plane-shear inclusion, and cylindrical Couette flow
evidence_signal: The model was benchmarked against analytical or known solutions and reproduced fracture patterns, displacement fields, and the expected linear crack-speed scaling from Griffith theory.
watch_item: The approach is highly nonlinear and validated on simplified test cases, so performance in fully realistic sea-ice conditions may depend on numerical resolution and calibration.
paper_title: A phase-field modeling approach to sea-ice fracturing
